Code fragment: public final TableField<OtpRecord, String> SENDTO = createField(DSL.name("sendTo"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR(100).defaultValue(DSL.inline("NULL", SQLDataType.VARCHAR)), this, "");
Explanation: The column 'sendTo' has a default value of "NULL" as a string literal instead of an actual NULL value, which can cause confusion and incorrect comparisons. Use SQLDataType.VARCHAR(100).nullable(true) without a default value, or use a proper NULL default.

Code fragment: public final TableField<OtphistoryRecord, String> OTPID = createField(DSL.name("otpId"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR(50).defaultValue(DSL.inline("NULL", SQLDataType.VARCHAR)), this, "");

The fetch(table, condition) method retrieves all columns from the table, which can lead to unnecessary data transfer and performance issues. Consider using selectFrom with explicit column selection or select(fields).from(table).where(condition) to fetch only the required columns.

The fetch(table, condition) method retrieves all columns from the table before mapping to the target type. Consider using select(fields).from(table).where(condition).fetchInto(type) with explicit column selection to improve performance.
